last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

lines: Context : 1: {i : int, s, pi, x, r, x0, r0 : block, p, c : block list}

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

where: setup before the frontier (positions 1–5.2) — absorb with `sp`/`wp`: 5 setup statement(s): pi <- nth witness p i; x <- Block.(-) s pi; DoubleQuery.ba
toolbox: `case: (<which guard>)` — split the divergent branch (YOU pick the condition).
`rcondt{i} N` / `rcondf{i} N` — force a branch (YOU pick t/f); for a loop guard, `while(true); auto`.
`swap [a..b] c` — line up statement order across the two sides.
`wp` / `wp -N -N` — absorb suffix statements (from the end) before `call`/`sim`.
`conseq(:_==> ={<the few equal vars>})` then `sim` — weaken to the equal prefix and auto-close it.
deep_focus.yours: which condition to `case` on, which way each guard resolves, the sample coupling (e.g. `t0{1} = t1{2}`), the smt lemmas.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier; sampling steps are visible inside the frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.right_first: no matching right-side sample at this frontier
first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The visible frontier is one-sided; use a one-sided tactic, alignment step, or seq cut before applying a symmetric tactic.

rows.left: 5 setup statement(s): pi <- nth witness p i; x <- Block.(-) s pi; DoubleQuery.bad <- true; ... (2 more)
rows.right: no right-side setup before this frontier
rows.proof_read: The left side has setup before the shared frontier; relate it to the precondition or carry the initialized state in the next invariant/cut.

rows.right: no matching right-side sample at this frontier
rows.proof_read: This is a sampling frontier; choose a coupling or distribution argument that connects the sampled values.

rows.right: no matching right-side statement at this frontier
rows.proof_read: Only the left side has this frontier statement; account for it with the precondition, a one-sided transform, or the next invariant.

rows.right: no matching right-side statement at this frontier
rows.proof_read: Only the left side has this frontier statement; account for it with the precondition, a one-sided transform, or the next invariant.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

left.note: count from frontier alignment; the body parser under-counted the asymmetric (right-only) setup statements (2 parsed)

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 5 0 / wp -5 -0 absorbs the straight-line setup (5 left, 0 right) before the next frontier

branch_guards.rcond_forms: rcondt{1} 3 or rcondf{1} 3 (direction needs guard entailment — yours to decide)
rcondt{1} 5 or rcondf{1} 5 (direction needs guard entailment — yours to decide)

checks: Keep one-sided instrumentation as a map, not as a tactic recipe.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis sees no live call sites after a prior `inline *` at tactic 12. Continue at procedure/ambient layer, or undo if a call lemma is needed.
One-sided sampling residual: r0{1} <$ dBlock. The postcondition still mentions the sampled value or a quantified witness, so this may be a true losslessness side condition or leftover coupling debt.

items.label: Before latest call-obligation tactic #36
items.why_checkpoint: call-obligation local boundary; selecting it keeps the opened call obligation and removes later local work
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #36 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After seq opened / before branch work #23
items.why_checkpoint: seq-local branch boundary; selecting it keeps the seq cut and removes branch-local work after it
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #23 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After call opened / before obligation work #7
items.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #7 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before call invariant #6
items.committed_tactic: call (_: DoubleQuery.bad, ={DoubleQuery.qs} /\ (DoubleQuery.bad{1} <=> DoubleQuery.bad{2}) /\ fdom PRFi.m{1} = DoubleQuery.qs{1}, DoubleQuery.bad{1} <=> DoubleQuery.bad{2}).
items.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #6 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before seq cut #22
items.committed_tactic: seq 3 3 : (={i, p} /\ 0 <= i{1} <= size p{1} /\ (DoubleQuery.bad{1} <=> DoubleQuery.bad{2}) /\ (!DoubleQuery.bad{2} => c{1} = c{2} /\ s{1} = s{2} /\ DoubleQuery.qs{1} = DoubleQuery.qs{2} /\ fdom PRFi.m{1} = DoubleQuery.qs{1})).
items.why_checkpoint: seq-cut boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this cut was committed
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #22 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before inline expansion #12
items.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #12 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: No fresh recommendation is available; parse the current goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: The current procedure frontier exposes a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, while, seq, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid one-sided hoare/phoare loop form.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

reference.note: Panels collapsed for focus. The essentials for THIS phase are in the focus above; the rest were noise here (a flat view of all of them is what disorients). If you genuinely need a precise field from one, ask with `inspect_context`.

surface_profile.name: l4_checked_action_surface
surface_profile.description: L4 Verifier-Checked Action Surface: exposes checked previews, post-probe goals, route health, recovery diagnosis, recovery signals, and selected diagnostics while demoting accepted partial openers that only create residual surgery.
